name: Glazed Roast Lamb with Rhubarb Salsa
recipeIngredient: Leg of lamb (4-6 lbs), boned and rolled
Fresh or frozen sliced rhubarb (6 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the 3 tablespoons honey, garlic salt, and 1/4 teaspoon ground pepper with 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar for the glaze.
2. Place the leg of lamb on a rack in a roasting pan. Brush the meat generously with the glaze mixture.
3. Roast at 325°F (160°C) for two to four hours, or until the internal temperature reaches 150°F (65°C) for medium-rare or 160°F (71°C) for medium. Brush with additional glaze occasionally during cooking.
4. For the rhubarb salsa, combine chopped onions, raisins, 1/2 cup honey, 2 tablespoons red wine vinegar, chopped jalapeno pepper, and minced garlic in a large saucepan.
5. Stir in ground cardamom and add sliced rhubarb (fresh or frozen).
6. Bring to a boil over medium heat, then reduce the heat and let simmer, covered, for 10 minutes.
7. Uncover and continue simmering for an additional 5 minutes to slightly reduce the liquid. Stir only if needed to avoid scorching.
8. Allow the rhubarb salsa to cool to room temperature before serving as a side dish with sliced roast lamb.
9. Store any leftovers in the refrigerator.
